{{Short description|none}} {{EngvarB|date=February 2023}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2023}} {{Infobox film festival | name = 2023 Cannes Film Festival | image = 2023 Cannes Film Festival.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 76th Cannes Film Festival featuring actress [[Catherine Deneuve]] during the shooting of ''[[La Chamade (film)|La Chamade]]'' (1968) | opening = ''[[Jeanne du Barry (film)|Jeanne du Barry]]'' | closing = ''[[Elemental (2023 film)|Elemental]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | founded = 1946 | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[Anatomy of a Fall]]'' | host = | number = 21 (In Competition) | date = 16–27 May 2023 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2022 Cannes Film Festival|2022]] | next =[[2024 Cannes Film Festival|2024]] |artistic_director=[[Thierry Frémaux]]}} The '''76th annual [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 16 to 27 May 2023.{{cite web |title=Cannes 2023: accreditations are open! |url=https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/infos-communiques/info/articles/cannes-2023-accreditations-are-open |website=[[Cannes Film Festival]] |access-date=24 February 2023 |date=3 February 2023 |archive-date=24 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230224041532/https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/infos-communiques/info/articles/cannes-2023-accreditations-are-open |url-status=live }} Swedish filmmaker [[Ruben Östlund]] served as jury president. With the French film ''[[Anatomy of a Fall]]'' winning the [[Palme d'Or]], the festival's top prize, [[Justine Triet]] became the third female director to achieve the prize, after [[Jane Campion]] in [[1993 Cannes Film Festival|1993]], and [[Julia Ducournau]] in [[2021 Cannes Film Festival|2021]].{{Cite news |last=Murray |first=Miranda |date=2023-05-28 |title=France's Triet becomes third female director to win Cannes' top prize |language=en |work=Reuters |url=https://www.reuters.com/lifestyle/wes-anderson-ken-loach-among-contenders-cannes-film-festivals-top-prize-2023-05-27/ |access-date=2023-05-28}} The official poster for the festival featuring actress [[Catherine Deneuve]] was created by Lionel Avignon and Stefan de Vivies of the design studio Hartland Villa from a photo taken by Jack Garofalo during the shooting of ''[[La Chamade (film)|La Chamade]]'' (1968). The poster was chosen to pay tribute to Deneuve for her contributions to film.{{Cite web |last=Goodfellow |first=Melanie |date=20 April 2023 |title=Cannes Film Festival Unveils 2023 Poster |url=https://deadline.com/2023/04/cannes-film-festival-2023-poster-catherine-deneuve-1235331733/ |access-date=19 April 2023 |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |language=en-US |archive-date=19 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230419183106/https://deadline.com/2023/04/cannes-film-festival-2023-poster-catherine-deneuve-1235331733/ |url-status=live }} During the festival, two [[Honorary Palme d'Or]] were awarded: the first one during the opening ceremony for [[Michael Douglas]], and the second one was awarded on short notice for [[Harrison Ford]] before the world premiere of ''[[Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny]]''.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-18 |title='Indiana Jones' swings into Cannes Film Festival; Harrison Ford honored before joyous festivalgoers |url=https://apnews.com/article/indiana-jones-dial-destiny-cannes-premiere-c94df09d6299a181eb2d713cc1959746 |access-date=2023-05-19 |website=AP NEWS |language=en |archive-date=19 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230519090552/https://apnews.com/article/indiana-jones-dial-destiny-cannes-premiere-c94df09d6299a181eb2d713cc1959746 |url-status=live }} The festival opened with ''[[Jeanne du Barry (film)|Jeanne du Barry]]'' by [[Maïwenn]]{{Cite web |title=Maïwenn's Jeanne du Barry to open the 76th Festival de Cannes |url=https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/press/press-releases/jeanne-du-barry-by-maiwenn-will-open-the-76th-festival-de-cannes/ |access-date=7 April 2023 |website=Festival de Cannes |date=5 April 2023 |language=en-US |archive-date=14 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230514124513/https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/press/press-releases/jeanne-du-barry-by-maiwenn-will-open-the-76th-festival-de-cannes/ |url-status=live }}{{Cite news |last=Jacobs |first=Julia |date=5 April 2023 |title=Johnny Depp Film About Louis XV Will Open Cannes Film Festival |language=en-US |work=The New York Times |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/04/05/movies/johnny-depp-cannes-film-festival.html |access-date=7 April 2023 |issn=0362-4331 |archive-date=17 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230517112537/https://www.nytimes.com/2023/04/05/movies/johnny-depp-cannes-film-festival.html |url-status=live }} and closed with [[Pixar]]'s ''[[Elemental (2023 film)|Elemental]]'' by [[Peter Sohn]].{{cite web |title=Elemental, the 27th Film by Pixar Animation Studios, to be the "Last Screening" at the 76th Festival de Cannes |date=19 April 2023 |url=https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/press/press-releases/elemental-the-27th-film-by-pixar-animation-studios-to-be-the-last-screening-at-the-76th-festival-de-cannes/}}{{Cite web |last=Azad |first=Navid Nikkhah |title=Elemental, the 27th Film by Pixar Animation Studios, to be the "Last Screening" at the 76th Festival de Cannes – Deed News |url=https://www.deed.news/news/press-release/elemental-the-27th-film-by-pixar-animation-studios-to-be-the-last-screening-at-the-76th-festival-de-cannes/ |access-date=19 April 2023 |website=www.deed.news |date=19 April 2023 |language=en-US |archive-date=19 April 2023 The Cannes Film Festival allocates a €15,000 grant for the winner of the First Prize, €11,250 for the winner of the Second Prize and €7,500 for the winner of the Third Prize.
